'''Elliot Minchella''' (born 28 January 1996) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} or {{rlp|lf}} for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. Minchella has also played for the [[Sheffield Eagles]] in the second tier, and the [[Bradford Bulls]] in [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]] and the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

On the 7th June 2025 he became only the second Hull KR captain to lift the Challenge Cup after the late [[Roger Millward]] in 1980, as his side defeated [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] 8-6 at [[Wembley Stadium]].

On 18th September 2025, he captained Hull KR to their second major trophy of the 2025 season as The Robins won the Super League League Leaders Shield for the first time in their history.

On the 11th October 2025, he cemented himself as a Hull KR immortal winning their 3rd major trophy of the year, beating Wigan in the Grand Final. Resulting in a treble winning season, and their first ever grand final win.

==Background==
Minchella was born in [[Bradford]], West Yorkshire, England.

==Playing career==
===Leeds Rhinos===
Minchella made his Super League début for [[Leeds Rhinos]] in 2013, scoring a try in a victory over Salford in his only appearance on the year. 

He made a further five appearances in 2014 before an off-field incident involving him and club mate [[Zak Hardaker]] being involved in an assault put him out of favour at Leeds.<ref name="YP281020">{{cite news |last1=Craven |first1=Dave |title=Hull KR's Elliot Minchella determined to make most of second Super League chance |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-krs-elliot-minchella-determined-make-most-second-super-league-chance-3017103 |access-date=6 April 2021 |work=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk |date=28 October 2020 |language=en}}</ref>

=== London Broncos (loan)===
In 2015 Minchella was  loaned to [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] club [[London Broncos]] for whole of the season. He played in 18 games in total and scored four tries and kicked a drop goal.<ref>{{cite web |title=Elliot Minchella joins London Broncos on loan from Leeds |url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12040/9784412/elliot-minchella-joins-london-broncos-on-loan-from-leeds |website=Sky Sports |access-date=6 April 2021 |date=1 April 2015}}</ref>

=== Sheffield Eagles===
He then signed for the [[Sheffield Eagles]] and in two seasons played 56 times and scored 21 tries for the club.<ref name="YP281020"/>

=== Bradford Bulls===
Minchella signed for home town side the [[Bradford Bulls]] on a 2-year deal on 21 September 2017.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/15550646.New_Bulls_signing_Minchella_comes_back_home_at_last/|title=Bradford Bulls signing Elliot Minchella comes back home at last as he signs two-year deal|publisher=Telegraph and Argus|date=22 September 2017|access-date=12 December 2017}}</ref>

===Hull KR===
At the end of the 2018 season, Minchella signed a three-year full time deal with Bradford, but in 2019 he was one of five players Bradford sold to Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref name="TA100321">{{cite news |last1=Rowan |first1=Newman |title=Former Bulls star handed captaincy duties at Super League club |url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/19147868.ex-bulls-player-elliot-minchella-made-hull-kr-vice-captain/ |access-date=6 April 2021 |work=Bradford Telegraph and Argus |date=10 March 2021}}</ref> In his two seasons at Bradford he made 59 appearances scoring 45 tries and becoming a regular goalkicker scoring 37 goals.<ref name="TA100321"/>

The curtailed 2020 season saw him limited to just 16 appearances for Hull KR and in the second game of the 2021 season he suffered an [[anterior cruciate ligament]] injury which ruled him out for the rest of the season.<ref>{{cite news |title=Hull KR's Minchella to miss rest of season |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/56649995 |access-date=6 April 2021 |work=BBC Sport |date=6 April 2021}}</ref>

On 12 August 2023, Minchella played for Hull Kingston Rovers in their 17-16 golden point extra-time loss to [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]] in the Challenge Cup final.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66463438|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull KR 16-17 Leigh Leopards - Leigh claim first Wembley final win in 52 years|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

Minchella played 23 matches for Hull Kingston Rovers in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished fourth on the table and qualified for the playoffs. He played in the club's semi-final loss against Wigan.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/live/rugby-league/67008107|title=Relive Wigan's thumping win v Hull KR to head to Grand Final|work=BBC Sport |date=6 October 2023 }}</ref>

On 12 October 2024, Minchella played in Hull Kingston Rovers [[2024 Super League Grand Final]] loss against Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2024/oct/12/wigan-v-hull-kr-super-league-grand-final-2024-live-score-updates?page=with:block-670aaacb8f087ed1d5a8a350/|title=Wigan v Hull KR: Super League Grand Final 2024 – as it happened|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

On 7 June 2025 he became the first captain of Hull KR since Roger Millward in 1980 to lift the Challenge Cup following their 8-6 win over Warrington Wolves.

On 7 June 2025 he became only the second Hull KR captain to lift the Challenge Cup after the late [[Roger Millward]] in 1980, as his side defeated [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] 8-6 at [[Wembley Stadium]]. On 18 September 2025, he captained Hull KR to their second major trophy of the 2025 season as they won the Super League League Leaders Shield for the first time in their history. On 9 October 2025, Minchella played in Hull Kingston Rovers 2025 Super League Grand Final victory over Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2jw497jpzo/|title=Hull KR beat Wigan in Grand Final to complete treble|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

On 19 February 2026, Minchella played in Hull Kingston Rovers [[World Club Challenge]] victory against [[Brisbane Broncos|Brisbane]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/live/cwy6dlgx3kyt/|title=Relive Hull KR's World Club Challenge win over Brisbane|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===International ===
He made his {{rlnt|England}} début on 29 June 2024 v {{rlnt|France}} in Toulouse.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=29 June 2024|title=France v England|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP4465137}}</ref>
